question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 15.00% on 2026-04-09 for the Metaculus question "Will the US and Iran agree to a ceasefire before May 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP for "Will the US and Iran agree to a ceasefire before May 2026?" will be strictly above 15% on April 9. The CP is currently exactly at the threshold (15.00%). The US and Iran are in active war since Feb 28, 2026, with structurally incompatible positions: Iran calls US proposals "unrealistic" while Trump claims progress. Key catalyst: Trump's April 6 deadline for energy strikes occurs 3 days before resolution. Time-decay pressure (May 1 deadline approaching) pushes CP downward, but high volatility (observed ±11pp weekly swings), Trump's tendency to extend deadlines and claim progress, and active mediation efforts create meaningful upside probability. Scenario-based Monte Carlo gives ~43.5%. The CP only needs to move infinitesimally above 15%, but the structural drift is downward.

factors.description: CP is exactly at threshold (15.00%) - symmetric starting point, any upward movement resolves YES
Time decay: as May 1 deadline approaches without a deal, rational forecasters should reduce probability. Only ~30 days remain for formal bilateral ceasefire.
Iran firmly rejecting US proposals as 'unrealistic, illogical and excessive' - positions structurally incompatible according to analysts
Active military escalation continuing (strikes on Tehran, orphanage struck, tanker attacked, Strait of Hormuz closed)
Trump's April 6 deadline for energy strikes is a major catalyst before April 9 resolution - creates high volatility but direction uncertain
Trump's optimistic rhetoric ('pretty sure of a deal') and tendency to extend deadlines could keep forecasters from lowering CP too much
Active mediation by Pakistan/Saudi/Turkey/Egypt provides diplomatic channel that could generate positive headlines
CP recently bounced from 11% to 15% (upward momentum), but also dropped from 22% to 11% (showing high volatility in both directions)
Manifold market for ceasefire by end of March 31 at <1%, confirming very low underlying probability of actual ceasefire

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.43 (43% probability that CP will be above 15% on April 9)**

Key reasoning:

- **Starting point**: CP is exactly at the 15.00% threshold — a knife's edge where any tiny upward movement resolves YES.
- **Downward pressure**: The war is ongoing with active escalation. Iran is firmly rejecting US proposals. As the May 1 deadline approaches, rational forecasters should be lowering estimates (time decay). The structural drift favors NO.
- **Upward potential**: Trump's April 6 energy-strike deadline (3 days before resolution) is a major catalyst — he's previously extended such deadlines while claiming progress. Active four-nation mediation creates opportunity for positive headlines. The CP only needs to be infinitesimally above 15%.
- **High volatility**: The CP has shown ±11pp weekly swings, meaning large moves in either direction are plausible over 9 days.
- **Net assessment**: Time-decay and escalation create modest downward drift from the 50/50 starting point, yielding ~43%.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42472) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-09 07:29:27 is higher than 15.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 15.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-09 07:29:27, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
